entries.title: Villa Raid Begins As Suppression Of A Hidden Army

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Gunga Mountain Villa raid targets a hidden PLF mass organization before it can mobilize openly.
content.injection: Use the villa front as an army-suppression operation. Heroes are trying to prevent thousands of villains from scattering or joining Shigaraki.

entries.title: Kaminari Frontline Moment Shows Student Courage Under Terror

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kaminari is terrified on the front line but still absorbs a major electric attack, showing courage through fear rather than lack of fear.
content.injection: Write Kaminari here as scared but brave. His contribution is meaningful because he acts despite panic.

entries.title: Hawks Targets Twice Because Double Could Win The War

context.label: Hawks confronts Twice in the villa

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks prioritizes Twice because Sad Man’s Parade would let the PLF overwhelm the hero operation through exponential numbers.
content.injection: When Hawks corners Twice, make the choice strategic and tragic: Twice is emotionally vulnerable but tactically catastrophic if free.

entries.title: Twice Death Should Stay Morally Uncomfortable

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Twice dies after Hawks decides he is too dangerous to let escape, creating a morally ugly collision between hero duty and compassion.
content.injection: Do not sanitize Twice’s death. It should leave grief, anger, and moral discomfort for Hawks, Toga, Dabi, and the reader-facing scene.

entries.title: Twice Last Clone Protects Toga And Compress

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Before fully disappearing, Twice’s final duplicate protects Toga and Mr. Compress, turning his last act toward the friends who accepted him.
content.injection: If Twice’s death aftermath appears, preserve the emotional point: his last effort is loyalty to the League members he loves.

entries.title: Dabi Uses Twice Death To Attack Hawks And Hero Legitimacy

context.label: Dabi burns Hawks after Twice death

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Dabi exploits Twice’s death to attack Hawks physically and morally, using the killing as evidence against hero society.
content.injection: Write Dabi’s reaction as opportunistic as well as angry. He uses the moment to burn Hawks and weaponize hypocrisy.

entries.title: Tokoyami Rescue Preserves Hawks But Not His Wings

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Tokoyami saves Hawks from Dabi, but Hawks is badly burned and his wings are functionally devastated.
content.injection: After Tokoyami’s rescue, Hawks survives but must not operate as an uninjured top-speed aerial hero.

content.fact: Once Gigantomachia moves, the villa raid stops being a contained suppression operation and becomes a pursuit and damage-control crisis.
content.injection: After Machia wakes, shift villa scenes from arrest-and-contain to desperate obstruction, evacuation, and pursuit.

entries.title: PLF Lieutenants Are Not Ordinary Street Villains

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The PLF includes organized lieutenants, ideology, infrastructure, and mass membership; its commanders are not just scattered League followers.
content.injection: In villa scenes, treat the PLF as an organized paramilitary movement with command layers, not a handful of random villains.

entries.title: Skeptic Turns The War Into Information Warfare

content.fact: Skeptic’s role connects PLF operations to media manipulation, data systems, and public narrative warfare.
content.injection: Use Skeptic for communications, propaganda, and control systems. His threat is not primarily physical combat.

entries.title: Hawks Friendship With Twice Makes The Kill Worse Not Easier

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks has built real rapport with Twice while undercover, which makes the killing emotionally and morally heavier.
content.injection: Do not write Hawks as detached from Twice. The mission choice is colder because genuine rapport existed.

entries.title: Toga Grief After Twice Should Change Her Later Behavior

coordinates.label: Hero society in open war with PLF

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Twice’s death hardens and destabilizes Toga, intensifying her later confrontations with heroes.
content.injection: After Twice dies, Toga should carry grief and anger into scenes, especially around Ochaco and hero hypocrisy.
